entities.name: V
entities.context: Vrijen Attawar, founder of Careerspan, demonstrating the platform to Jaya

entities.name: Jaya
entities.context: Recipient of Careerspan demo; will use the employer-side candidate landing view and share links with candidates

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; platform that scores candidate fit using story-based inputs beyond résumés

entities.name: Candidate Landing View
entities.context: Employer-facing screen showing bottom-line summary, elevator pitch, and strengths/weaknesses for each candidate

entities.name: Story-Based Inputs
entities.context: Structured behavioral prompts that candidates complete to generate richer signal than a résumé alone

entities.name: Two-Story Minimum
entities.context: Careerspan rule requiring candidates to answer at least two structured behavioral prompts before fit scoring

entities.name: Vibe Checks
entities.context: Feature allowing candidates to check fit against roles; pre-loads the employer's role when arriving via shared link

entities.name: Before/After Comparison
entities.context: Demo technique V uses to show how much more signal a story-rich candidate profile provides versus a bare résumé

relationships.context: V advises Jaya not to over-optimize the structure if it feels roughly right
V is the founder demonstrating Careerspan's capabilities
Jaya will use Careerspan's employer-side views and shared links to receive candidates
The candidate landing view is a core employer-facing feature of Careerspan
Careerspan drives its candidate views from story-based inputs rather than résumés alone
Careerspan enforces a two-story minimum so the system can meaningfully score fit
Vibe checks are a candidate-side feature letting them assess fit against roles
V demonstrates the value of stories by showing the same candidate with and without story data
V believes story-rich profiles provide substantially more hiring signal than résumés alone
